recipes.name: Earnings Reviewer
recipes.description: Review public earnings materials for a company or ticker, then save a sourced research report with finance guardrails and human-review language.

recipes.name: Month-End Close Assistant
recipes.description: Turn exported close data into a human-reviewed close checklist, exception register, reconciliation checks, and follow-up plan.

recipes.name: GL Reconciliation Reviewer
recipes.description: Review exported ledger or reconciliation rows for unmatched items, duplicates, timing differences, outliers, and missing support.

recipes.name: Cashflow Snapshot
recipes.description: Summarize exported cash activity into inflow/outflow themes, pressure points, watch items, and operator follow-ups.

recipes.name: Budget Variance Reviewer
recipes.description: Review exported budget-versus-actual rows for material variances, likely drivers, timing issues, and owner follow-ups.

recipes.name: AP Duplicate Payment Reviewer
recipes.description: Review exported AP bills or payment rows for duplicate-looking invoices, repeated payments, and vendor exceptions.

recipes.name: AR Aging Collections Prioritizer
recipes.description: Prioritize exported AR aging or invoice rows by overdue balances, customer risk, concentration, and follow-up urgency.

recipes.name: Web Research Brief
recipes.description: Search the web for a topic, extract useful pages, synthesize a sourced research brief, and save it as an artifact.

recipes.name: MCP Web Research
recipes.description: Fetch a URL through the MCP fetch workflow, summarize it with AI, save the result, and notify the operator.

recipes.name: CSV to Report
recipes.description: Paste CSV rows and produce a compact analysis report with statistics, patterns, caveats, and recommended next questions.

recipes.name: Webhook Change Monitor
recipes.description: Poll a URL, detect meaningful content changes, summarize the difference, and save a monitoring artifact.

recipes.name: Blog Post from Outline
recipes.description: Expand a rough topic or outline into a structured blog post with an editorial polish pass and saved markdown output.

recipes.name: Meeting Agenda Builder
recipes.description: Turn a meeting description into a timed agenda with owners, discussion blocks, decisions, and follow-up actions.

recipes.name: Rough Notes to Document
recipes.description: Transform messy notes or bullet fragments into a clean markdown document with headings, structure, and clear takeaways.

recipes.name: Translate & Save Both Versions
recipes.description: Translate input text, preserve the original, and save the translated version as an artifact for review or reuse.

recipes.name: Article Audio Summary
recipes.description: Fetch an article URL, summarize the important points, and convert the summary into an audio artifact.

recipes.name: Podcast from Text
recipes.description: Polish provided text into a podcast-style script and create an audio version for review or distribution.

recipes.name: Code Security Review
recipes.description: Read a source file and produce a focused security and bug-risk review with findings and suggested fixes.

recipes.name: Codebase Pattern Report
recipes.description: Search a codebase for a pattern, collect matching context, and save an AI-analyzed report for refactor planning.

recipes.name: Run Shell & Summarize
recipes.description: Execute a local shell command, summarize the output in plain English, and surface the result for quick review.

recipes.name: Todo Focus Briefing
recipes.description: Analyze current todos and produce a short daily focus plan with priorities, blockers, and next actions.

recipes.name: Weekly Status Report
recipes.description: Pull todo activity into a weekly status report with completed work, in-flight items, risks, and next-week focus.

recipes.name: Draft Email Replies
recipes.description: Fetch unread Gmail messages, draft concise replies, save them for review, and keep human approval in the loop.

recipes.name: Daily Slack Summary
recipes.description: Turn current todos into a standup-style summary and post it to a Slack channel when Slack is configured.
